- What test file(s) to create or modify

- What implementation file(s) to create or modify

## Test Coverage Guidelines

- Cover the happy path for every feature
- Cover edge cases (empty inputs, boundary values, invalid data)
- Cover error handling (what happens when things fail)
- For async operations, test both success and failure paths

## Execution Order

For each subtask, always:
1. Write the test(s) first
2. Run the tests to confirm they fail (red)
3. Implement the feature

5. Refactor if needed, re-run tests

54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}

2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}

83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored your Kanban board codebase. Here's the complete picture:

### Project Structure

**Root Directory**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/`

Monorepo with two workspaces:
- `client/` - React frontend with Vite
- `server/` - Express.js backend with WebSocket support

### Client Dependencies (package.json)

The client currently has **minimal dependencies**:
- `react@^18.3.1`
- `react-dom@^18.3.1`
- **No state management library (Redux, Zustand, Context)**
- **No drag-and-drop library (dnd-kit not yet installed)**

Development dependencies include Vitest for testing and Vite for bundling.

### Component Structure

#### Board Component
**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx`

- Top-level component rendering three `<Column>` components (Ready, In Progress, Done)
- Uses `useBoard()` hook for state management
- Opens `CardModal` when card clicked
- No existing drag-and-drop handling
- Props passed to columns: `title`, `cards` array, `onCardClick` callback

#### Column Component
**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx`

- Renders semantic `<section>` with `aria-label`
- Maps cards to `CardTile` components
- Props: `title`, `cards` array, `onCardClick`
- CSS class: `column-cards` (flex column layout)
- No drop zone or drag handling

#### CardTile Component
**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx`

- Individual card display (draggable target for dnd-kit)
- Currently: role="button", keyboard accessible (Enter/Space)
- Props: `card` object, `onCardClick` callback
- Renders: title, assignee, optional description
- No draggable attributes

#### CardModal Component
**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`

- Portal-rendered modal for card details
- Editable fields: title, assignee
- Read-only: description
- Comments section with `CommentList` component
- Delete confirmation dialog
- Integrated with `onUpdate`, `onDelete`, `onAddComment` callbacks

### State Management

**Custom Hook**: `useBoard()` 
**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`

**State structure**:
```javascript
{
  cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] },
  loading: boolean,
  error: string | null
}
```

**Key features**:
- Optimistic updates with rollback pattern (updateCard, deleteCard)
- Temp-ID optimistic pattern (createCard, addComment)
- WebSocket integration via `useWebSocket()` hook
- Automatic state synchronization on WS reconnect
- Event suppression to avoid echoing local actions
- `cardsRef` for synchronous state reads

**Public API methods**:
- `createCard(data)` - Creates optimistically with temp ID
- `updateCard(id, data)` - Rolls back on error (column changes blocked)
- `deleteCard(id)` - Rolls back on error
- **`moveCard(id, targetColumn, position)`** - Key for drag-drop, rolls back on error
- `addComment(cardId, data)` - Optimistic with temp ID

**Column naming convention**:
- State keys: `ready` | `in_progress` | `done` (underscore)
- API values: `ready` | `in-progress` | `done` (hyphen)
- Conversion via `columnToKey()` helper function

### API Layer

**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/api/client.js`

Implemented functions:
- `fetchCards()` - GET /api/cards
- `createCard(data)` - POST /api/cards
- `updateCard(id, data)` - PATCH /api/cards/:id (supports position field)
- `deleteCard(id)` - DELETE /api/cards/:id
- `createComment(cardId, data)` - POST /api/cards/:cardId/comments

**Note**: No explicit `moveCard` API function, but `updateCard()` supports `{ column, position }` payload

### Server API Endpoints

**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/server/api/cards.js`

Routes:
- `POST /api/cards` - Create card
- `PATCH /api/cards/:id` - Update card fields (title, assignee, column, description, position)
- `DELETE /api/cards/:id` - Delete card
- `PATCH /api/cards/:id/move` - **Dedicated move endpoint** (expects `{ column, position }`)

**Broadcast events** (WebSocket):
- `card:created`
- `card:updated`
- `card:deleted`
- `card:moved`

### Database & Move Logic

**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/server/db/queries.js`

**moveCard function**:
```javascript
moveCard(id, column, position)
```

Algorithm:
1. Validates card exists
2. Uses transaction for atomic operations
3. Handles position using fractional positioning strategy
4. For gap >= 0.001: position = (before + after) / 2
5. For tight gaps: renormalizes all positions in column (1.0, 2.0, 3.0, ...)
6. Excludes moved card from siblings calculation

**Card schema**:
- `id` (UUID)
- `title` (string, required)
- `assignee` (string | null)
- `column` (string: 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done')
- `position` (float for fractional positioning)
- `description` (string | null)
- `created_at` (timestamp)
- `comments` (array, joined at fetch time)

### Testing Infrastructure

**Test files**:
- `/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` - Board component tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)
- `/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` - Column tests
- `/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` - CardTile tests
- `/kanban/client/src/hooks/useBoard.test.js` - Comprehensive hook tests with mocked API/WS

**Test patterns**:
- Mock `useBoard` hook in component tests
- Mock API calls and WebSocket in hook tests
- Use `renderHook`, `act`, `waitFor` from testing-library
- Mock implementations use `vi.fn()`, `vi.mock()`, `mockResolvedValue()`, `mockReturnValue()`

### WebSocket Integration

**File**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-13/kanban/client/src/hooks/useWebSocket.js`

- Real-time event streaming from server
- Auto-reconnection logic
- Event handlers: `onEvent(eventType, payload)`
- Tracked events: `['card:created', 'card:updated', 'card:deleted', 'card:moved', 'comment:created']`

### CSS Structure

**Board layout** (`Board.css`):
- Flexbox layout with gap, responsive (flex-direction: column on mobile)

**Column styling** (`Column.css`):
- `flex: 1` with min-width: 240px
- Light background (#e8eaf0)
- Card gap: 0.5rem
- `.column-cards` - flex column container for cards

**CardTile styling** (`CardTile.css`):
- Individual card styling (ready for drag overlay/ghost effects)

### Key Observations for dnd-kit Integration

1. **No existing drag-drop**: CardTile is not draggable, Column has no drop zone
2. **Excellent API readiness**: `moveCard()` already exists with position + column support
3. **WebSocket-ready**: Real-time sync via `card:moved` events
4. **Optimistic updates**: Hook supports rollback if API fails
5. **State structure**: Cards grouped by column - perfect for dnd-kit's droppable zones
6. **Position strategy**: Fractional positioning in DB avoids expensive reordering
7. **Keyboard accessibility**: CardTile already keyboard accessible (needs updating for dnd-kit)
8. **No state management library**: useBoard hook sufficient, but consider adding dnd-kit context

### Missing for dnd-kit Implementation

1. dnd-kit dependencies not installed
2. CardTile needs `draggable` attributes (or dnd-kit sensors)
3. Column needs `droppable` zone setup
4. Move logic integration between dnd-kit events and `moveCard()` hook
5. Visual feedback: drag preview, drop indicators, overlay effects
6. Tests for drag-drop interactions
7. Accessibility: keyboard support for dnd-kit (currently using mouse/touch)

---

All files are production-ready with comprehensive testing. The architecture is clean and modular, making dnd-kit integration straightforward. The `moveCard()` function with position + column parameters is perfectly designed for fractional positioning that dnd-kit enables.

1	import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
2	import {
3	  fetchCards,
4	  createCard as apiCreateCard,
5	  updateCard as apiUpdateCard,
6	  deleteCard as apiDeleteCard,
7	  createComment,
8	} from '../api/client.js'
9	import { useWebSocket } from './useWebSocket.js'
10	
11	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
12	// Module-level helpers
13	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	
15	/**
16	 * Map API column value → state key.
17	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
18	 * @param {string} col
19	 * @returns {string}
20	 */
21	export function columnToKey(col) {
22	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
23	}
24	
25	/**
26	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
27	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
28	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
29	 */
30	function groupCards(cards) {
31	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
32	  for (const card of cards) {
33	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
34	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
35	  }
36	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
37	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
38	  }
39	  return board
40	}
41	
42	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
43	
44	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)
45	let _tempId = 0
46	function nextTempId() {
47	  return '__temp_' + (++_tempId)
48	}
49	
50	// WebSocket constants (module-level — computed once on import)
51	const WS_URL = `${location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss:' : 'ws:'}//${location.host}/ws`
52	const WS_EVENTS = ['card:created', 'card:updated', 'card:deleted', 'card:moved', 'comment:created']
53	
54	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	// useBoard hook
56	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	
58	/**
59	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
60	 *
61	 * Column convention
62	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
63	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
64	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
65	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
66	 *
67	 * @returns {{
68	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
69	 *   loading: boolean,
70	 *   error: string|null,
71	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
72	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
73	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
74	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
75	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,
76	 * }}
77	 */
78	export function useBoard() {
79	  const [cards, setCards] = useState(EMPTY_BOARD)
80	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
81	  const [error, setError] = useState(null)
82	
83	  // Counter for concurrent in-flight operations so loading stays true
84	  // until the LAST operation finishes.
85	  const pendingRef = useRef(0)
86	
87	  // Ref that always holds the latest cards state so we can read it
88	  // synchronously (functional updaters are called lazily by React, which
89	  // would leave a rollback variable null if the API rejects as a microtask
90	  // before the updater runs).
91	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)
92	
93	  // Suppression map — consume-once with 5s TTL to ignore WS echo of local actions
94	  const suppressedRef = useRef(new Map())
95	
96	  // Tracks whether we've had at least one successful connection (for reconnect detection)
97	  const hasConnectedRef = useRef(false)
98	
99	  /**
100	   * Apply a state update eagerly: compute the next value synchronously from
101	   * cardsRef.current, update the ref, then call setCards with the plain
102	   * computed value (not a function) so React picks it up on the next render.
103	   */
104	  function applyCards(updater) {
105	    const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(cardsRef.current) : updater
106	    cardsRef.current = next
107	    setCards(next)
108	  }
109	
110	  function beginOp() { pendingRef.current++; setLoading(true) }
111	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }
112	
113	  function suppressWsEvent(id) {
114	    suppressedRef.current.set(id, Date.now() + 5000)
115	  }
116	
117	  function consumeSuppression(id) {
118	    const expiry = suppressedRef.current.get(id)
119	    if (expiry === undefined) return false
120	    suppressedRef.current.delete(id)
121	    return Date.now() <= expiry
122	  }
123	
124	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	  // WebSocket event handler (stub — filled in Subtask 2)
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	  // useCallback([]) is safe: applyCards/consumeSuppression close only over stable refs
129	  const handleWsEvent = useCallback((eventType, payload) => {
130	    switch (eventType) {
131	      case 'card:created': {
132	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
133	        const key = columnToKey(payload.column)
134	        applyCards(prev => {
135	          if (prev[key].some(c => c.id === payload.id)) return prev
136	          return {
137	            ...prev,
138	            [key]: [...prev[key], { ...payload, comments: payload.comments ?? [] }]
139	              .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position),
140	          }
141	        })
142	        break
143	      }
144	      case 'card:updated': {
145	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
146	        // Remove from current column, re-insert in correct column.
147	        // Handles both in-place updates AND cross-column moves via PATCH /api/cards/:id.
148	        // Preserves existing comments (WS payload does not include comments array).
149	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
150	        applyCards(prev => {
151	          let existingComments = []
152	          let found = false
153	          const next = {}
154	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
155	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
156	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
157	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
158	          }
159	          if (!found) return prev
160	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
161	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
162	          return next
163	        })
164	        break
165	      }
166	      case 'card:deleted': {
167	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
168	        applyCards(prev => {
169	          const next = {}
170	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
171	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
172	          }
173	          return next
174	        })
175	        break
176	      }
177	      case 'card:moved': {
178	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
179	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
180	        applyCards(prev => {
181	          const next = {}
182	          let existingComments = []
183	          let found = false
184	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
185	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
186	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
187	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
188	          }
189	          if (!found) return prev
190	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
191	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
192	          return next
193	        })
194	        break
195	      }
196	      case 'comment:created': {
197	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
198	        applyCards(prev => {
199	          const next = {}
200	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
201	            next[k] = col.map(c =>
202	              c.id === payload.card_id
203	                ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, payload] }
204	                : c
205	            )
206	          }
207	          return next
208	        })
209	        break
210	      }
211	    }
212	  }, [])
213	
214	  const { status: wsStatus } = useWebSocket(WS_URL, { onEvent: handleWsEvent, events: WS_EVENTS })
215	
216	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	  // Reconnect reconciliation — re-fetch on WS reconnect to recover missed events
218	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	  useEffect(() => {
221	    if (wsStatus === 'connected') {
222	      if (hasConnectedRef.current) {
223	        // Reconnect detected — re-fetch to reconcile any missed events
224	        let cancelled = false
225	        fetchCards()
226	          .then(data => { if (!cancelled) applyCards(groupCards(data)) })
227	          .catch(() => {}) // silent fail; don't disrupt existing state
228	        return () => { cancelled = true }
229	      }
230	      hasConnectedRef.current = true
231	    }
232	  }, [wsStatus])
233	
234	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	  // Initial data load
236	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
237	
238	  useEffect(() => {
239	    let cancelled = false
240	    setError(null)
241	    beginOp()
242	
243	    fetchCards()
244	      .then(data => {
245	        if (cancelled) return
246	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
247	        endOp()
248	      })
249	      .catch(err => {
250	        if (cancelled) return
251	        setError(err.message)
252	        endOp()
253	      })
254	
255	    return () => { cancelled = true }
256	  }, [])
257	
258	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
260	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
263	    const tempId = nextTempId()
264	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
265	    const key = columnToKey(col)
266	
267	    const optimisticCard = {
268	      id: tempId,
269	      title: data.title,
270	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
271	      column: col,
272	      position: Infinity,
273	      description: data.description ?? null,
274	      created_at: Date.now(),
275	      comments: [],
276	    }
277	
278	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
279	    beginOp()
280	
281	    try {
282	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
283	      suppressWsEvent(created.id)
284	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
285	      applyCards(prev => {
286	        const next = { ...prev }
287	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
288	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
289	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
290	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
291	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
292	        return next
293	      })
294	      return created
295	    } catch (err) {
296	      applyCards(prev => ({
297	        ...prev,
298	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
299	      }))
300	      throw err
301	    } finally {
302	      endOp()
303	    }
304	  }, [])
305	
306	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
307	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
308	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
309	
310	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
311	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
312	    const safeData = { ...data }
313	    delete safeData.column
314	
315	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
316	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
317	    suppressWsEvent(id)
318	    applyCards(prev => {
319	      const next = {}
320	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
321	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
322	      }
323	      return next
324	    })
325	    beginOp()
326	
327	    try {
328	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
329	      applyCards(prev => {
330	        const next = {}
331	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
332	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
333	        }
334	        return next
335	      })
336	      return updated
337	    } catch (err) {
338	      applyCards(rollback)
339	      throw err
340	    } finally {
341	      endOp()
342	    }
343	  }, [])
344	
345	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
346	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
347	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
348	
349	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
350	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
351	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
352	    suppressWsEvent(id)
353	    applyCards(prev => {
354	      const next = {}
355	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
356	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
357	      }
358	      return next
359	    })
360	    beginOp()
361	
362	    try {
363	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
364	    } catch (err) {
365	      applyCards(rollback)
366	      throw err
367	    } finally {
368	      endOp()
369	    }
370	  }, [])
371	
372	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
373	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
374	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
375	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
376	
377	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
378	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
379	
380	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
381	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
382	    suppressWsEvent(id)
383	    applyCards(prev => {
384	      let cardToMove = null
385	      const next = {}
386	
387	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
388	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
389	        if (idx !== -1) {
390	          cardToMove = col[idx]
391	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
392	        } else {
393	          next[k] = col
394	        }
395	      }
396	
397	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
398	
399	      const movedCard = {
400	        ...cardToMove,
401	        column: targetColumn,
402	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
403	      }
404	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
405	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
406	      return next
407	    })
408	    beginOp()
409	
410	    try {
411	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
412	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
413	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
414	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
415	      applyCards(prev => {
416	        const next = {}
417	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
418	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
419	        }
420	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
421	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
422	        return next
423	      })
424	      return updated
425	    } catch (err) {
426	      applyCards(rollback)
427	      throw err
428	    } finally {
429	      endOp()
430	    }
431	  }, [])
432	
433	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
435	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
436	
437	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
438	    const tempId = nextTempId()
439	    const optimisticComment = {
440	      id: tempId,
441	      card_id: cardId,
442	      author: data.author,
443	      content: data.content,
444	      created_at: Date.now(),
445	    }
446	
447	    applyCards(prev => {
448	      const next = {}
449	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
450	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
451	          c.id === cardId
452	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
453	            : c
454	        )
455	      }
456	      return next
457	    })
458	    beginOp()
459	
460	    try {
461	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
462	      suppressWsEvent(comment.id)
463	      applyCards(prev => {
464	        const next = {}
465	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
466	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
467	            c.id === cardId
468	              ? {
469	                  ...c,
470	                  comments: c.comments
471	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
472	                    .concat(comment),
473	                }
474	              : c
475	          )
476	        }
477	        return next
478	      })
479	      return comment
480	    } catch (err) {
481	      applyCards(prev => {
482	        const next = {}
483	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
484	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
485	            c.id === cardId
486	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
487	              : c
488	          )
489	        }
490	        return next
491	      })
492	      throw err
493	    } finally {
494	      endOp()
495	    }
496	  }, [])
497	
498	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
499	  // Public interface
500	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
501	
502	  return {
503	    cards,
504	    loading,
505	    error,
506	    createCard,
507	    updateCard,
508	    deleteCard,
509	    moveCard,
510	    addComment,
511	  }
512	}
